raw_response:
The tongue is formed during embryonic development through various processes involving different regions. The arytenoids, which form part of the larynx rather than the tongue itself, develop separately as arytenoid cartilages derived from pharyngeal arches. Therefore:

Answer D: Arytenoid swellings Your analysis accurately identifies that the arytenoid swellings contribute to forming structures other than the tongue, specifically parts of the larynx. Thus, option D (Arytenoid swellings) is correct because they do not participate in the formation of the tongue. Answer D: Arytenoid swellings
